question: How does the NuScale containment differ from a conventional large dry containment?

nuscale_ch01_0189.title: BTP 6-2, Rev 3: Minimum Containment Pressure Model for PWR ECCS PerformanceAll (B.1 thru B.3)Various
nuscale_ch01_0189.summary: This section reviews NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria related to containment pressure and water chemistry for PWR ECCS performance. It concludes that certain criteria are not fully applicable to the NuScale design due to differences such as the absence of a containment spray system, sump, or secondary containment, and that the minimum containment pressure model (BTP 6-2, Rev 3) does not apply since a LOCA in NuScale does not result in core uncovery. However, relevant pH and hydrogen generation guidelines are still considered applicable where appropriate.

nuscale_ch01_0014.title: Acronyms and Abbreviations
nuscale_ch01_0014.summary: This section explains the conventions used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR) for numbering tables, figures, and pages, as well as the handling of proprietary and sensitive information. Tables and figures are numbered according to their section, and sensitive content is withheld from the public version in accordance with regulatory requirements.

nuscale_ch01_0188.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.3 Hydrogen Generation from Aluminum Corrosion
nuscale_ch01_0188.summary: The section reviews NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding pH control and hydrogen generation from aluminum corrosion in emergency coolant water. While NuScale’s design lacks certain systems referenced in the criteria (such as containment spray systems and sumps), the intent of the guidelines is still met due to the collection and recirculation of ECCS water within the containment vessel. Other criteria, such as those related to minimum containment pressure and dual containment leakage, are deemed not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0167.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.1 Primary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0167.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3, as its containment vessel does not include fission product clean-up or pressure suppression systems; instead, fission product control is achieved through its passive, submerged containment design. Aspects of the guidance related to secondary containment and other fission product control systems are not applicable, since NuScale does not use these features. Only the primary containment vessel and its isolation systems are credited for fission product control in the NuScale Power Plant.

nuscale_ch01_0168.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.2 Secondary Containment
nuscale_ch01_0168.summary: The NuScale design does not include secondary containment or fission product clean-up systems, as its passive safety features and submerged containment vessel inherently control fission products. Therefore, the regulatory criteria in SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3 related to secondary containment and certain fission product control systems are not applicable to NuScale, and only specific containment parameters such as leakage rates are relevant for review.

nuscale_ch01_0035.title: Fuel Handling and Reactor Maintenance Areas
nuscale_ch01_0035.summary: The fuel handling and reactor maintenance areas are located at the west end of the Reactor Building (RXB) and include spaces for the spent fuel pool (SFP), refueling pool, and dry dock, facilitating safe handling and storage of both new and spent fuel assemblies. These areas are designed for efficient fuel transfer, maintenance operations, and provide radiation protection for personnel, with equipment such as cranes and pallet jacks supporting fuel movement and storage activities.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios and reduce reliance on external power, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional light water reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0022.title: Engineered Safety Feature Materials
nuscale_ch01_0022.summary: The NuScale reactor’s containment vessel (CNV) is kept under vacuum during normal operations, which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits oxygen for safety. The design relies on passive safety features—such as natural heat removal and elimination of large external coolant loops—which lowers the risk of core damage and improves overall safety compared to traditional nuclear plants.

nuscale_ch01_0165.title: SRP 6.5.1, Rev 4: ESF Atmosphere Cleanup SystemsII First full paragraph on Page 6.5.1-6, Design, Testing, and Maintenance of ESF Atmosphere Cleanup System Air Filtration and Adsorption Units
nuscale_ch01_0165.summary: The NuScale Power Plant design does not utilize engineered safety feature (ESF) filter or ventilation systems for mitigating the consequences of a design basis accident. Instead, any existing nonsafety-related HVAC system with filtering is not credited in the dose analysis, making the regulatory guidance on ESF atmosphere cleanup system air filtration and adsorption units not applicable to NuScale.

nuscale_ch01_0113.title: Figure 1.7-3b: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0113.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing standard symbols and abbreviations for valve positions, actuator types, and valve status during normal operation. It explains the meaning of terms such as normally open/closed, fail open/closed, and describes various actuator mechanisms and their representations on the diagrams. This ensures consistent interpretation of P&IDs throughout the documentation.

nuscale_ch01_0171.title: SRP 6.5.5, Rev 1: Pressure Suppression Pool as a Fission Product Cleanup SystemAll Various
nuscale_ch01_0171.summary: The section explains that SRP 6.5.5, which addresses the use of a pressure suppression pool as a fission product cleanup system, is not applicable to the NuScale reactor design because NuScale does not use or credit a suppression pool, a feature relevant only to large LWRs like BWRs. Therefore, the associated regulatory criteria do not apply to NuScale’s safety analysis.

nuscale_ch01_0187.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.2 Spray Water pH and Water Chemistry Requirements for Fission Product Removal
nuscale_ch01_0187.summary: The NuScale design partially conforms to certain regulatory criteria regarding pH and water chemistry for emergency coolant and fission product removal, as it does not use a containment spray system or sump, but ECCS water is still recirculated within the containment vessel. Some criteria, such as those related to minimum containment pressure and dual containment leakage, are not applicable to NuScale because its design differs from traditional large PWRs and does not include features like secondary containment or scenarios leading to core uncovery.

nuscale_ch01_0114.title: Figure 1.7-3c: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0114.summary: This section provides the legend for piping and instrumentation diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It lists and defines the standard symbols for various equipment such as tanks, vessels, pumps, compressors, heat exchangers, filters, HVAC components, and other miscellaneous devices, ensuring consistent interpretation of engineering drawings.

nuscale_ch01_0169.title: SRP 6.5.3, Rev 3: Fission Product Control Systems and StructuresII.4 Other Fission Product Control Systems
nuscale_ch01_0169.summary: The NuScale design does not include traditional fission product clean-up or secondary containment systems found in conventional LWRs; instead, fission product control is achieved through its passive, submerged containment vessel. As a result, many SRP 6.5.3 criteria related to these systems are not applicable, and the only credited engineered safety feature (ESF) for fission product control is the containment vessel with its isolation features.

nuscale_ch01_0048.title: Security Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0048.summary: The Security Buildings (SCBs) at a NuScale Power Plant are nonsafety-related structures that include facilities such as the primary access control building. These buildings support plant security functions but are not classified as safety-related structures within the plant’s overall design.

nuscale_ch01_0002.title: Introduction
nuscale_ch01_0002.summary: This section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under 10 CFR 52. It outlines the scope of the report, describing the NuScale Power Module (NPM) design, its modular and scalable features, and key safety characteristics such as passive shutdown, compact containment, and advanced digital controls. The FSAR covers both individual modules and multi-module plant configurations.

nuscale_ch01_0050.title: Diesel Generator Buildings
nuscale_ch01_0050.summary: The Diesel Generator Buildings (DGBs) in the NuScale Power Plant each house a single backup diesel generator and their auxiliary equipment, but do not contain any safety-related systems or support engineered safety functions (ESFs). Their primary purpose is to provide support and housing for the generators, associated equipment, HVAC system, and maintenance area.

nuscale_ch01_0003.title: Plant Location
nuscale_ch01_0003.summary: The section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification and details the design, safety analysis, and regulatory compliance of the NuScale Power Module (NPM). The NPM is a modular, scalable nuclear steam supply system that can operate from one to twelve modules within a single facility, featuring advanced safety, compact design, and digital controls.

nuscale_ch01_0005.title: Reactor Type
nuscale_ch01_0005.summary: The NuScale reactor is a small modular pressurized water reactor (PWR) featuring a cylindrical steel containment vessel designed to ASME standards, with internal pressure maintained at a vacuum during normal operation. Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) integrates the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel, uses natural circulation for cooling (eliminating the need for pumps), and delivers approximately 50 MWe per module, with up to 12 modules per plant for a total net output of about 570 MWe.

nuscale_ch01_0060.title: NuScale Integral System Test Program
nuscale_ch01_0060.summary: The NuScale Integral System Test Program was established to generate thermal-hydraulic data for system characterization and to validate safety analysis codes using a scaled model of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) design. The NIST-1 facility replicates key reactor systems and provides essential data for validating proprietary safety codes, supporting both safety methodology development and the Design Certification Application.
